message:
feat(beads): enhance implementation tasks with detailed API specifications

Second review pass adding comprehensive Rust API specifications to key
implementation tasks across the 15 new feature epics.

## Tasks Enhanced with Full API Specs

### Drag-and-Drop Framework (bd-1csc)
- bd-1csc.1: Draggable trait, DragPayload struct, DragConfig thresholds
- bd-1csc.2: DropTarget trait, DropPosition enum, DropZone wrapper

### Terminal Capability Simulator (bd-k4lj)
- bd-k4lj.2: Capabilities bitflags, CapabilityProfile struct, predefined
  profiles (xterm-256, xterm, vt100, dumb, screen), ProfileBuilder

### Contextual Help System (bd-2qbx)
- bd-2qbx.2: TooltipConfig, TooltipPosition enum, WithTooltip wrapper,
  automatic positioning avoiding screen edges

### Focus Management (bd-1n5t)
- bd-1n5t.2: FocusManager full API with focus/blur/navigate methods,
  FocusGroup for Tab boundaries, FocusTrap for modals, runtime integration

### Notification/Toast System (bd-3tmk)
- bd-3tmk.2: NotificationQueue with FIFO ordering, priority handling,
  deduplication, stacking behavior, QueueConfig

### Undo/Redo Framework (bd-1nac)
- bd-1nac.2: HistoryManager with dual stacks, merge logic for consecutive
  typing, memory budgeting, branch handling (redo cleared on new action)

### Modal/Dialog System (bd-39vx)
- bd-39vx.3: ModalStack for nested modals, z-index management, input
  routing to top modal only, backdrop dimming for lower modals

### Internationalization (bd-ic6i)
- bd-ic6i.4: BidiSegment using unicode-bidi crate, visual reordering,
  cursor movement in mixed LTR/RTL text

### Reactive Bindings (bd-2my1)
- bd-2my1.2: bind!/bind_two_way! macros, BindingBuilder pattern,
  BindingHandle for lifecycle management

## API Spec Content

Each enhanced task now includes:
- Complete Rust struct/trait definitions with doc comments
- Implementation code snippets showing key algorithms
- Integration patterns with existing FrankenTUI systems
- Named unit tests covering happy path and edge cases
- Snapshot test names for visual verification
- File location within crate structure

## Validation

- No dependency cycles (verified via bv --robot-insights)
- All cross-epic dependencies intact
- Internal subtask sequencing preserved

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
